description: A vertical designed graphic combining a news-outlet headline header with a photograph. The top portion is a white band carrying the maroon 'EdNC' (EducationNC) logo and a bold black serif headline: 'U.S. Department of Education moves many programs to other agencies ‘to break up federal bureaucracy’'. The lower two-thirds is a color photograph of Donald Trump, in a dark navy suit, white shirt and red striped tie, standing and clapping/gesturing inside an industrial facility (overhead crane, large rolled steel coils on a yellow rack at right marked '07'). Behind him a crowd of supporters in orange high-visibility jackets and hard hats cheer and photograph him; a blue banner at right reads 'AMERI...' (America). A faint circular agency watermark sits at lower left. The composition uses the borrowed-credibility technique of pairing a real third-party news headline with a triumphant rally image of the President among industrial workers, dramatizing the dismantling of the Department of Education and transfer of programs to other agencies. Caption framing criticizes 'failed bureaucracy' and declining test scores. Target audience: voters and workers supportive of shrinking federal government and boosting workforce development.
notable_text: U.S. Department of Education moves many programs to other agencies ‘to break up federal bureaucracy’; EdNC
